communications.subject: FOIA Request :: R007519-040326
communications.communication:
The Office of the President of the Cook County Board of Commissioners is in receipt of your request for information under the Freedom of Information Act (the "Act"). Your request has been received in this office on 4/3/2026 and given the reference number R007519-040326 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

Dear FOIA Officer, 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I am requesting access to public records held by the Cook County Department of Environment and Sustainability regarding the development project at 112 E. Burlington Ave, La Grange, IL 60525 (the "Jackson Square" project). 

Specifically, I am requesting the following records created or received between September 1, 2024, and March 16, 2026: 

Demolition & Asbestos Permits: Any and all permit applications, approvals, or waivers for demolition or asbestos abatement. This includes, but is not limited to, the Demolition Debris Diversion (3D) Plan, asbestos inspection reports, and any "Letter of Approval" or "Letter of Waiver" issued for this site. 

Correspondence: Any and all email correspondence, text messages, and internal memoranda regarding this property. Please search for communications involving department staff and the following external parties: the Village of La Grange, Five South Six, LLC, and any environmental consultants or demolition contractors associated with the project. 

Inspection Records: Any reports or notes from site inspections, environmental complaints, or violation notices related to this address. 

Fee Waiver Status: 
I am requesting a waiver of all fees as this request is not for a commercial purpose. I am seeking this information to monitor and report on environmental compliance and government transparency regarding a significant local development. 

Please provide these records in an electronic format to this email address. If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation citing the specific legal exemptions. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Concerned Citizen of La Grange 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2257868/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 
Thank you, 
Rachel

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request #R007519-040326
communications.communication:
Subject: RE: FOIA Request # R007519-040326 – Second Extension Not Authorized

Dear Ms. Dailey,

I am in receipt of your message from April 17, 2026, requesting a second extension of time to respond to my FOIA request.

Please be advised that under 5 ILCS 140/3(e), a public body is permitted to unilaterally extend the response time only once for a period not to exceed 5 business days. Your office has already utilized this statutory extension, which expired on April 17, 2026.

Under the Act, any further extension beyond the initial 10 business days requires a written agreement between the requester and the public body. I do not agree to an additional extension.

As the statutory deadline has passed without a production of records, Cook County is now in violation of the Act. Per 5 ILCS 140/3(d), please note that the public body:

May not impose any fees for the records provided; and

May not treat this request as "unduly burdensome."

I expect the responsive records to be produced immediately. If a full response is not received by the end of business on April 21, 2026, I will most likely proceed with a Request for Review through the Illinois Attorney General’s Public Access Counselor (PAC) and/or review the situation with an attorney to consider filing a lawsuit.

I am happy to work with the agency, but I am not understanding the difficulty behind the request and why more than 2 weeks of time is required to produce the results.    

Sincerely,
Concerned Citizen of La Grange

title: Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) Records Request (Berkeley Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) Records Request (Berkeley Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

We request the following records related to Automated License Plate Readers ("ALPRs") and your law enforcement agency ("Agency").
Part A - Information related to ALPR data sharing.
A1. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shared ALPR data in 2025;
A2. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency received ALPR data in 2025;
A3. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information in 2025;
A4. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information in 2025.
The information requested in items A1-A4 is readily available for extraction through the ALPR software you use. For example, with Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, this data can be exported easily using the Data Sharing Report function. With Flock Safety, this information is available through the transparency portal function.
Most agencies have encountered little difficulty in providing this information (see below). In a small number of cases, an agency has interpreted items A1-A4 as requesting the creation of a record that does not exist. We disagree: this data is maintained by your software, can be exported through software your agency has already purchased, and is therefore subject to CPRA.
However, should you disagree, you may alternatively produce the following records:
A5. All agreements, memoranda of understanding, requests, or other certifying documents that external agencies have signed to comply with Sec 1798.90.52(b) for accessing your agency's ALPR data. These documents are also often required under the "Releasing ALPR Data" subsection of a California law enforcement agency's SB 34 policy.
A6. All agreements, memoranda of understanding, requests, or other certifying documents that your agency has signed pursuant to Sec 1798.90.52(b) to access other agencies' ALPR data.
A7. All "records of access" required by Sec. 1798.90.52(a) for the periods June 5th, 2024 until the date of processing this request.
Items A5-A7 are not necessary if your agency provides the information requested in items A1-A4. We feel that items A1-A4 are more focused and would require less labor from your agency to produce.
Part B - Information Related to Numbers of “Detections” (plate scans) and “Hits” (plate scans that matched to a hotlist)
B1. The aggregate number of "detections" collected during 2025.
B2. The aggregate number of “hits” during 2025.
B3. The aggregate number of "detections" from June 5th, 2024 until the date this request is processed.  If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. For example, most Flock Safety systems may not have this data beyond 30 days.
B4. The aggregate number of “hits” from June 5th, 2024 until the date this request is processed. If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. 
B5. Average Daily Scans based on the past amount of days in which you retain data.
The information requested in items B1-B4 is readily available through the ALPR software you use. For example, with Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, this data can be exported easily using the Hit Ratio Report function. With Flock Safety, this information is available through the transparency portal function. As noted above, this information exists within your system and can be easily exported with existing software.
Part C - Assistance, Description, and Recommendations.
We make the following requests under section 6253.1 of the California Public Records Act
C1. A description of the information technology and physical location in which the records exist.
C2. Assist us identifying records and information that will help the public understand how ALPR data and hotlists are shared, how much data is collected year-to-year, and how much of that data matches a hot list; and
C3. Provide suggestions for overcoming any practical basis for denying access to the information sought.
Please note that more than 80 California law enforcement agencies have provided the information requested in Parts A1-A4 and B1-B4 in previous years. For your reference we have included a PDF below with the names of the agencies that have historically provided these records.
(This URL links to a dataset with links to the records: https://www.eff.org/document/data-driven-2-california-dragnet-data-set)
Part D - Information Related to New Changes
D1. Flock Safety has announced that they are building a product called Flock Nova, which combines information from agencies into one place, such as “Unify CAD, RMS, video, LPR, and public records.” It is mentioned in this article (https://www.404media.co/license-plate-reader-company-flock-is-building-a-massive-people-lookup-tool-leak-shows/) that the tool is being used by some law enforcement agencies in an early access program (https://www.flocksafety.com/devices/flock-nova#:~:text=Flock%20Nova%20Early%20Access%20Now%20Open). Is/was your Agency part of, invited to, or booked a demo for the early access program? Is/has your Agency utilized Flock Nova? If yes, have you used the tool in any police investigations?
D2. Please send a document of the most up-to-date ALPR policy that your Agency uses.
Part E – Information Related to ALPR Camera Locations
E1. The location (address, intersection, coordinates, or nearest landmark) of each ALPR camera operated, maintained, or accessed by the Agency as of the date of this request.
E2. If your agency uses mobile ALPR cameras (e.g., on patrol cars), please indicate how many mobile units are currently deployed, and describe any policies that govern how locations of scans from mobile units are logged or stored.
E3. If exact addresses or coordinates are withheld, please provide general placement descriptions (e.g., "fixed camera at Main St & 2nd Ave," or "mobile ALPR unit assigned to Northern District patrol vehicle").
This information is typically accessible within ALPR system management software. For example:
- In Flock Safety camera location information is visible through the camera dashboard and deployment maps.
- In Vigilant/Motorola, camera locations are often listed in the device management or configuration settings.
Part F - ALPR Vendor Contracts and Purchase Agreements
F1. All contracts, purchase orders, service agreements, statements of work, or memoranda of understanding between the Agency and any vendor of ALPR technology (including Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions/Motorola, etc.) in effect during January 1, 2024–June 5, 2025. 
F2. All invoices, receipts, and payment records related to the purchase, installation, operation, or maintenance of ALPR systems during January 1, 2024–June 5, 2025. 
F3. Any grant applications or grant award documents (e.g., from DHS, DOJ, or private foundations) that funded or supported the acquisition or expansion of ALPR systems.
Part G – Internal Communications and Rationale for Placement
G1. Any internal memos, emails, reports, or presentations from 2022–2025 discussing where ALPR cameras should be placed or were ultimately placed. This includes communications from officers, command staff, city officials, or vendor representatives regarding:
- Placement location recommendations
- Crime statistics or justifications used to determine placement
- Community concerns or opposition
- Equity or privacy impacts
- “High crime” area designations or related targeting rationale
G2. Any communications with Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions, or other ALPR vendors from 2022–2025 regarding camera placement, pilot programs, early access programs, or system expansion.
CPRA requires you to undertake reasonable efforts to locate responsive records and to work in good faith with requesters to respond to their request. See CYAC v. City of National City, 220 Cal.App.4th 1385, 1430 (2013). Thus under the CPRA you are obligated to conduct a reasonable search and cannot deny a request merely because it might generate a large volume of records.
We ask that you please respond to this request within 10 days either by providing all the requested records or by providing a written response setting forth the legal authority on which you rely in withholding or redacting any document, as well as stating when documents will be made available. Should you choose to withhold or reject this request, we ask that you provide an explanation of why your agency is responding differently compared to the many others that have complied with our request.
Part H – ALPR Audit Logs & Access Records (More Detail)
H1. A list of all ALPR database users employed by the Agency between June 5, 2024 and the present, including job titles, divisions, and access levels. If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. 
H2. Audit logs showing ALPR database access by Agency personnel from June 5, 2024 to the present. If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period.  Please include:
1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the date above, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.
- Date and time of access
- Name and Job Title of User
- Search parameters (e.g., plate number or geographic query)
- Stated reason for access, if logged
2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period from the date above, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.
These logs are required under California Civil Code § 1798.90.52(a) and can help determine how often ALPR data is being queried and by whom. These records have been disclosed by several agencies in prior CPRA requests.
If the records responsive to any part of this request are held by another department, division, or city office (such as the Mayor’s Office, Department of Technology, SFMTA, or a surveillance oversight board), please identify the appropriate custodian or refer this request accordingly, as required under Government Code § 6253.1.
In particular, if your agency does not maintain records related to:
- The policy rationale or decision-making process for camera placement
- Contracts or procurement related to ALPR vendors
- Surveillance technology policy
Please inform me which department or office may hold these records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Omar Solis

The City has identified responsive records. As a courtesy, a copy of those records is being provided with this message. Portions of these records have been redacted based upon the law enforcement investigatory privilege, the official information privilege, the confidentiality afforded certain ALPR data, and where the public interest in withholding personally identifying information of members of the public clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure. (Cal. Gov. Code § 7923.600; Cal. Evid. Code § 1040(a); Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.90.55(b); Cal. Gov. Code § 7922.000.) Unless otherwise noted, all redaction determinations have been made by the undersigned City staff member. Staff are in the process of compiling and reviewing additional responsive records and anticipate being able to provide all responsive records no later than Friday, December 19, 2025 at 5 p.m. Sincerely, Sam Harvey Deputy City Attorney ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Berkeley.

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Lebanon School District/SAU88)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Lebanon School District/SAU88)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: Re: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Lebanon School District/SAU88)
communications.communication:
Hello-

We are in receipt of your request.  We are working to compile the
information you request; you can expect a response within 10 business days.

Thanks,

Tim

-- 
Timothy Ball
Business Administrator
SAU88 (Lebanon, NH)

-- 
_*The Right-To-Know Law* provides that most email communications, to or 
from Lebanon School District employees regarding the business of the School 
District, are government records available to the public upon request. 
Therefore, this email communication may be subject to public disclosure. 
This email is intended solely for the person or entity to which it is 
addressed and may contain confidential and/or privileged information. Any 
review, dissemination, copying, printing, or other use of this email by 
persons or entities other than the addressee is strictly prohibited. If you 
receive this email in error, please notify the sender immediately and 
delete the material from any computer.
_

title: Cabell County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Cabell County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Drew Solt 
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
Mar. 12, 2026

To whom it may concern,

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Cabell County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting:
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 to today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 to today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.

The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.
Thank you for considering my request.

Sincerely,  Drew Solt 
740-243-6793
drewsolt@yahoo.com

communications.subject: Fw: March 13, 2026 FOIA Request
communications.communication:
________________________________
From: Beth Zerkle <bzerkle@cabellcounty.org>
Sent: Monday, March 30, 2026 12:18 PM
To: drewsolt@yahoo.com <drewsolt@yahoo.com>
Subject: March 13, 2026 FOIA Request


Good afternoon,



In response to your FOIA request dated March 13, 2026, “The custodian of any public records, unless otherwise expressly provided by statute, shall furnish proper and reasonable opportunities for inspection and examination of the records in his or her office and reasonable facilities for making memoranda or abstracts therefrom, during the usual business hours, to all persons having occasion to make examination of them.”  W. Va. Code § 29B-1-3(c) (emphasis supplied).



The Cabell County Commission office has received your FOIA request regarding opioid funds, which was sent to the Cabell County Assessor’s Office.  Below is a link to the website with the requested information:



https://cabellcounty.org/government/opioid_settlement_applications.php



Further, materials are available in the Commission office, where you may inspect and copy them (WV Code §29B-1-3) during regular business hours (8:30 am – 4:30 pm Monday – Friday) with five (5) business days' written notice.



Thank  you,

Beth Zerkle

County Administrator

Cabell County Commission

750 5th Avenue, Suite 309

Huntington, WV 25701

O 304-526-8634

M 681-294-5026

title: Pennsylvania Department of Environmental Protection Oil & Gas Permitting & Compliance Records Request

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Pennsylvania Department of Environmental Protection Oil & Gas Permitting & Compliance Records Request
Your Right-to-Know Law Request Has Been Received by DEP
communications.communication:
The Department of Environmental Protection's Right-to-Know Law (RTKL) Office has received your correspondence. Requests for records will be processed in accordance with the provisions of the RTKL, 65 P.S.§§67.101, et seq.

Requests received by this account after regular business hours(4:30 pm) or when the Office is otherwise closed pursuant to Management Directive 530.17 will be marked as received on the next business day.

Thank you for your Right-to-Know Law submission that will be forwarded to the Agency Open Records Officer (AORO) for processing. 

If you wish to modify a pending Right-to-Know Law request, do not complete another online form. A second online submittal will not modify your original request. Instead, please send an e-mail to ep-dep-rtk@pa.gov and we will assist you with modifying your original request.

Please note that your request is deemed received on the Department’s next business day if:
  •  Your request was submitted after 4:30 p.m. Monday-Friday, 
  •  Your request was submitted during a weekend, 
  •  Your request was submitted on a holiday observance recognized by the Commonwealth, or
  •  Your request was submitted any time Executive Offices are closed as a result of weather or any other emergency.

The Department will contact you no later than five business days from the receipt of your request as to its status. If you have any further questions on this process, please visit the Department’s webpage at:
https://www.dep.pa.gov/About/PublicRecords/Pages/RightToKnow.aspx

Thank you.

title: FDA on lecanemab

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FDA on lecanemab
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

July 15, 2025
Food and Drug Administration
Division of Freedom of Information (HFI-35)

RE:  Freedom of Information Act request

Dear FOIA Officer:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552a, I am requesting all FDA FAERS case  reports, including the narrative and summary reports on the two drugs below:
1.)  lecanemab aka: lecanemab-IRMB or Leqembi  for all times up to the most recent entries.
2.)  donanemab, aka donanemab-AZBT or Kisunla for all times up to the most recent entries.

I'm also requesting case report number 24075739 regarding a patient on lecanemab who experience “confusion”.

I am a professional journalist writing on commission for The BMJ and The Lever.
 
As an investigative journalist, I request a waiver of fees associated with processing this FOIA request and duplicating responsive records.  

Thank you for your attention to this request.

Sincerely,


Jeanne Lenzer

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jeanne Lenzer

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Warren County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Warren County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Incarcerated Firefighter Program Records (Department of Forestry & Fire Protection (CAL FIRE))

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Incarcerated Firefighter Program Records (Department of Forestry & Fire Protection (CAL FIRE))
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records from January 2020 to December 2025 of digital presentations, instructional guides, and curriculum outlines to incarcerated firefighter participants that is able to be unredacted for public use.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ricky Padilla

CAL FIRE

title: santa ana alpr audits follow up

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: santa ana alpr audits follow up
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Part A - Information related to ALPR data sharing.

A1. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data;
A2. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data;

A3. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information;
A4. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information.

The information requested in items A1-A4 is readily available for extraction through the ALPR software you use. For example, with Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, this data can be exported easily using the Data Sharing Report function. With Flock Safety, this information is available through the transparency portal function. Other software, such as BOSS, has similar functionality.

In a small number of cases, an agency has interpreted items A1-A4 as requesting the creation of a record that does not exist. We disagree: this data is maintained by your software, can be exported through software your agency has already purchased, and is therefore subject to CPRA.

However, should you disagree, you may alternatively produce the following records:

A5. All agreements, memoranda of understanding, requests, or other certifying documents that external agencies have signed to comply with Sec 1798.90.52(b) for accessing your agency's ALPR data. These documents are also often required under the "Releasing ALPR Data" subsection of a California law enforcement agency's SB 34 policy.
A6. All agreements, memoranda of understanding, requests, or other certifying documents your agency has signed under Sec 1798.90.52(b) to access other agencies' ALPR data.
A7. All "records of access" required by Sec. 1798.90.52(a) from 2019 until the date of processing this request.

Items A5-A7 are unnecessary if your agency provides the information requested in items A1-A4. We feel that items A1-A4 are more focused and would require less labor from your agency to produce.

Part B - Information Related to Numbers of “Detections” (plate scans) and “Hits” (plate scans that matched to a hotlist)

B1. The aggregate number of "detections" collected 2019 to today's date
B2. The aggregate number of “hits” 2019 to today's date

The information requested in items B1-B4 is readily available through the ALPR software you use. For example, with Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, this data can be exported easily using the Hit Ratio Report function. With Flock Safety, this information is available through the transparency portal function. Other software, such as BOSS, has similar functionality. As noted above, this information exists within your system and can be easily exported with existing software.


Part C-

Records of:
C1. Officers with authorized access to ALPR systems
C2. Any audits that include records for the reason of the search, misuse, and how many times the ALPR search was run.
C3. The number of times other agencies, including federal agencies, with access to the department's ALPR data.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Simran Patel

Rose Terse

title: 2026 Ohio (New York - Secretary Of State)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: 2026 Ohio (New York - Secretary Of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:
Pursuant to the Ohio Freedom of Information Law and the new 2026 Ohio Sunshine Laws Manual I hereby request the following records:
Pursuant to Section 149.43 of the Ohio Revised Code, I hereby request the following records:  unofficial copies of any documents of complaints and actions taken (any info allowable by law) filed with the Ohio Charitable Law and the Ohio Secretary of State regarding :


Congregation Sholom
DBA: Temple Sholom of Cincinnati (doing business as that has lapsed)
10828 Kenwood Rd. Cincinnati, OH 45242
EIN 31-0584318


Central Conference of American Rabbis - OH Registration #26991
EIN: 13-1769747
NY State Registration Number - 05-99-38
Recently changed address:
183 Madison Ave #1504
New York, NY 10016
Address listed with Charities Bureau:
355 LEXINGTON AVE 8th Floor, null
NEW YORK , NY 10017

Ohio Subsidiary
Ohio Entity ID 26991
1160 Dublin Road, Suite 400
Columbus OH 43215
Pursuant to Section 149.43 of the Ohio Revised Code & New York Public Officers Law § 87(2), I request access to and copies of all non-exempt public records in the possession, custody, or control of the Ohio Attorney General or any other public office relating to the Central Conference of American Rabbis & Temple Sholom (Cincinnati, Ohio), including but not limited to records connected to any complaints, investigations, or actions taken under Ohio Charitable Law.
If any portion of the requested records is withheld, I request that all reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions be provided, with redactions. 
This request specifically includes, and is not limited to:
All copies of any and all organizational and governing documents submitted to, filed with, or otherwise in the possession of any public office relating to Congregation Sholom (Temple Sholom of Cincinnati) and the Central Conference of American Rabbis, including but not limited to articles of incorporation or association, bylaws, constitutions, amendments, and any other governance or structural documents.
All complaints submitted against Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis and its representatives, attorneys, or agents, submitted to any public office, including responses to complaints, position statements, and supporting exhibits.
All documents, correspondence, and materials submitted by Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis and its representatives, attorneys, or agents to any public office, including responses to complaints, position statements, and supporting exhibits
All communications between Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis  (or its representatives) and any public office, including emails, letters, requests for information, follow-up communications, and meeting summaries.
All documents, records, or materials that were reviewed, relied upon, cited, or considered by any public office in connection with complaints, inquiries, or investigations involving Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis regardless of whether such materials originated from Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis or third parties.
All records created by any public office relating to Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis including but not limited to investigative summaries, factual findings, memoranda, recommendations, determinations, closure letters, or case status communications.

THE CENTRAL CONFERENCE OF AMERICAN RABBIS – DOS ID: 2510108 
0 Assumed Names 
1 Application of Authority Please be advised that copies of corporate/business entity documents listed above must be obtained by submitting a written request along with the appropriate fee to the New York State Department of State, Division of Corporations, One Commerce Plaza, 99 Washington Avenue, Suite 600, Albany, NY  12231. You can submit this request along with the appropriate fee in writing by mail, fax, or online. The written request should include the following information:  
  *  The exact name of the corporation or other business entity. 
  *  The DOS ID number or the exact date of formation or authorization. 
  *  The document(s) requested. 
  *  A statement as to whether the request is for plain copies or certified copies. 
  *  A statement as to whether the request is for routine processing or expedited processing. The mailing address where the copies are to be mailed. The fee for a plain copy of a document is $5 and the fee for a certified copy is $10.  The remittance should be made payable to the New York State Department of State.   The Department of State does not provide copies of filing receipts. Copy requests are usually processed within 7 business days. The Division of Corporations offers an optional service for the expedited processing of copy requests. For an additional fee of $25, the Division will process the request within 24 hours. For an additional fee of $75, the Division will process the request on the same day it is received; and for an additional $150 fee, the Division will process the request within 2 hours. You may obtain expedited processing by submitting a separate non-refundable fee and placing the notation on the envelope and cover letter, "Attention: Expedited Processing." You may submit your request via fax and pay the fee with a credit or debit card.  The Department of State’s Division of Corporations accepts MasterCard, Visa and American Express for the payment of fees.  To pay for a fee by using a credit or debit card complete and sign the Credit Card/Debit Card Authorization Form and submit it to the Division of Corporations with your request.  The Credit Card/Debit Card Authorization Form is available on our website at https://dos.ny.gov/copies-corporation-or-business-entity-documents You may fax requests to the Division of Corporations at (518) 473-1654. Please note that copies will be returned by first-class mail by the United States Postal Service. We do not provide these documents by fax or email. You may request that the document(s) be returned by overnight delivery service enclosing a prepaid shipping label with your written request. The Department of State will not accept prepaid shipping labels with the Department of State listed as the sender.  The prepaid shipping label must list yourself as the sender and the receiver.  You may obtain copies of filed documents online can be obtained by going directly to the Corporations Business Express site using the following link: https://www.businessexpress.ny.gov/app/answers/cms/a_id/3439/kw/copied  We hope you find the above information to be of assistance. Pursuant to Public Officers Law §89(4)(a) you have 30 days to submit a written appeal of this determination to: Department of State FOIL Appeals Officer One Commerce Plaza 99 Washington Avenue Albany, NY 12231 Sincerely, Records Access Office New York State, Department of State

title: 12-18-2025 Evanston Border Patrol Attack

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: 12-18-2025 Evanston Border Patrol Attack
communications.communication:
To the FOIA Officer of Evanston Police Department,

This request is made pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act for records sufficient to document any involvement actions observations communications or presence of Evanston Police Department personnel in connection with federal enforcement activity conducted by United States Customs and Border Protection within Evanston.

Incident date:
December 17 2025

Locations:
Mobil gas station at 1950 Green Bay Road Evanston Illinois
Home Depot at 2201 Oakton Street Evanston Illinois

Description:
Public statements issued by the City of Evanston and the Evanston Police Department acknowledge that Evanston Police responded to multiple incidents involving Customs and Border Protection (CBP) activity and that officers were present. Public reporting further describes Evanston Police officers supervisors and command staff on scene directing traffic interacting with federal agents and remaining present during detentions and community response. This request seeks records sufficient to document the actual scope and nature of Evanston Police Department involvement.

Specifically, I seek:

1) All unedited body worn camera recordings and associated audio captured by any Evanston Police Department officer present at the above locations for the full duration of their presence
2) Any dash camera, in vehicle Fleet camera, or similar recordings from Evanston Police Department vehicles present at or responding to the above locations
3) All dispatch records, computer aided dispatch logs, call for service records, and associated audio including any 911 calls related to these incidents
4) All records generated or received by the Evanston Police Department in connection with these incidents including incident reports supplemental reports investigative or informational reports supervisor notes approval or command level documents photographs videos attachments officer narrative supplements and any written or electronic communications referencing Customs and Border Protection or federal agents

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the case of any applicable fees, it is requested that an estimate of the total charges be provided prior to fulfilling this request. The preferred method of receipt is through electronic delivery.

Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. A response within 5 business days, as the statute requires, is anticipated.

With thanks,

David S. (he/him/his), Lucy Parsons Labs

communications.communication: Hello thanks. Please also be sure to include all dash cam footage as well.

communications.subject: Your City of Evanston public records request #25-4429 has been closed.
communications.communication:
City of Evanston ************************************************************************ Record request #25-4429 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Denial of Request Your Request is hereby Denied in its entirety. The reason(s) for denial are stated below: 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a) and 50 ILCS 706/10-20(b) In order for body-worn camera footage to be given under the act, the person must be the subject of the video, or a qualifying event must occur    You have a right to appeal this decision within 60 days to Leah Bartelt, Public Access Counselor, Office of the Illinois Attorney General, 500 South 2nd Street, Springfield, Illinois 62701, (phone number 1-877-299-3642 email public.access@ilag.gov) or to seek judicial review under Section 11 of FOIA, 5 ILCS 140/11. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Evanston.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.communication:

Sincerely,

Emma Best

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: 321D-IR-A7788
communications.communication:
Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on "Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request" on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@ic.fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI's FOIPA Program is searching the FBI's indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 134 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 523 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 1,335 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 1,875 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
Record/Information Dissemination Section (RIDS) FBI-Information Management Division
170 Marcel Drive, Winchester, VA 22602-4843
Direct: (540) 868-4593
Fax: (540) 868-4391
Questions E-mail: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

title: RAW RIPA Data (San Fernando Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: RAW RIPA Data (San Fernando Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello, 

I am requesting your department's state mandated RIPA stop data from 2020 to today's date. Please upload in .csv or Excel format. No PDFs. 

Thank you

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

title: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Burnsville  Police Department)

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Burnsville  Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident location, area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Police Data Request :: P010294-021726
communications.communication:
Dear Samuel : 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of the City of Burnsville. Your request was received in this office on February 17, 2026 and given the reference number P010294-021726 for tracking purposes. To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident location, area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2218752/ 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. We attempt to process all requests within 10 days, however this may be delayed depending on the number and complexity of requests we have received. If you have questions, please reply to this email or contact our office at 952-895-4607.

communications.subject: Police Data Request :: P010294-021726
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of February 17, 2026, Reference # P010294-021726. Dear Samuel , Thank you for submitting your public records request to the City of Burnsville on February 17, 2026. We reviewed our files and found records that match your request . You can log in to the Burnsville Data Request (FOIA) Center Hello Samuel, 
The data you’ve requested (everything we can provide at no charge) is now attached in our GovQA portal.  We are unable to upload to your portal in order to remain consistent with our internal record retention process. 
Please note that through the years, we have changed our process for documenting and tracking Use of Force incidents, so there are multiple files in different formats from the disparate systems we use. I worked with our systems vendor to extract the full amount of data we had in spreadsheet format from each system. 
Our retention for this data is 7 years, so unfortunately we don’t have any reports older than 1/1/2019. However we do have Use of Force summaries of past years, so I have attached those for all years you requested in case that’s helpful. 

Another note - We do not have “Area Occurred” (beat/patrol area) listed on these reports or in the summary documents.  I attached a map of what we consider our patrol areas if you would like to reference this in your research. 
For the cases where these spreadsheets and summaries are not sufficient, we are able to retrieve the original Use of Force reports (back to 2019) but that will require us to go into officer personnel files to download them individually and will cost $33 per hour of staff time, and will also require redaction of the fields that are not public data. Please let me know if this is something you’d like to proceed with. Thanks Holly Helm   
  
  
 Holly Helm | Police Services Manager 
100 Civic Center Parkway | Burnsville, MN | 55337 
952-895-4669 (direct)

title: Baton Rouge Police Department materials on Stalker VXE30 drone

communications.subject: Louisiana Public Records Law Request: Baton Rouge Police Department materials on Stalker VXE30 drone
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

The Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) is submitting this request for records under the Louisiana Public Records Act.

The requested records are those, for the period between January 1, 2025 and the date on which this request is processed, describing and relating to the Baton Rouge Police Department acquisition and use of Stalker VXE30 drones, which has been shared on the agency’s Facebook page and reported on by local media. 

Responsive records include, but are not limited to, contract agreements, payment materials, and use policies and logs related to BRPD’s deployment of the aforementioned drone. 

CONTRACT AND AGREEMENTS
• All contracts, memorandums of understanding, and any other written agreements between this agency and Edge Autonomy (or any other applicable entity) for equipment, training, and other elements related to this agency’s acquisition of the Stalker VXE30 drone. 

FINANCIAL MATERIALS
• All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations, including purchasing orders, related to equipment, training, and other elements included in this agency’s acquisition of the Stalker VXE30 drone. For reference, BRPD Police Chief TJ Morse recently told reporters: "The drone itself, of course, cost money, and then the training for all the pilots was a two-week training. The batteries and equipment, with everything together, it was about a $1,000,000 package deal, which was money that we got at the end of 2024, beginning of 2025. Working with the Mayor's office at the beginning of 2025, they gave us that money in our budget to be able to purchase the drone.”

DOCUMENTATION OF USE
• Any logs of use — including flight logs, the reasons for dispatch, flight times, flight paths, and any payloads used during the flight — documenting the deployment of the aforementioned drones. 

POLICIES OF USE
• All policies and procedures that apply to the use of the drone, including but not limited to all policies describing circumstances under which the drone may be deployed, all descriptions of where data may be uploaded or shared (including other software platforms or other agencies), the period of retention for any footage or data captured during the drone’s deployment, and all policies related to consequences related to the misuse of the drone or its capabilities

We ask that these materials be provided on a rolling basis; as your office collects and reviews records, we would appreciate it if your office would provide those materials to us incrementally. If possible, please provide any responsive contractual and financial materials within the statutorily-mandated public record law timeframe. We would prefer communications to be provided next. Please contact us for further discussion regarding the search and release of these materials.

This request is not being made for commercial purposes. This request is being made in the public interest. Please assess fees appropriately in accordance with a public records request being made in the public interest.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform us of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I request that all records be disclosed in their native, digital format and transmitted to us electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or by CD-ROM if not.

If you need any more information in order to process the request, or cannot process a portion of the request for any reason, please respond to the email address on this request. Further discussion may be conducted via email. Please let us know if a phone call will be necessary or desirable; such contact information will be provided upon request.

Thank you very much for your time and help.

Sincerely,

Beryl Lipton

communications.subject: RE: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Compensation Agreement for Anduril Arsenal-1
communications.communication:
Hi Trey,

I really appreciate your providing me with this agreement. May I ask two follow up questions?

One, pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, may I have a copy of the semi-annual PILOT Statement produced between January 1- March 1 of this year that specifies that the amount due for the first half of the annual Project Thor PILOT payment to the Teays Valley Local School District? If Teays Valley produced any objections to the calculation, could you kindly provide me with the objection?

Also, may I ask if you all have estimated what your revenue loss will be over 30 years due to the compensation agreement equaling 30% of the portion of real property taxes Teays Valley would have received had the CRA Exemption not been in place for the Arsenal-1 buildings? Ie, what amount does Teays Valley estimate that the full 100% would have been?

I really appreciate the consideration -- thank you very much. 

best

Taylor Barnes

title: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Merced County Registrar of Voters)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Merced County Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in your County in that year. In case it is helpful to see an example of the type of data I am requesting from your county, Alameda County has these records posted for the 2024 General Election on their Election Information for November 2024 under the name “Cast Vote Records (CVR) Report”.

Please let me know if you need any further clarification on the type of data that I am requesting.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

We will respond on or before March 16, 2026.

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Alger County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Alger County)
communications.communication:
Dear Alger County Clerk FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Alger County Clerk county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

title: Reocords Request: Suspected Falsified Documents to the EPA

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Reocords Request: Suspected Falsified Documents to the EPA
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

-A log of complaints, filed to the Environmental Protection Agency between January 1st, 2025 and the date of response to this request, concerning the suspected tampering, misrepresentation, or otherwise altering of documents, permits, certifications, or any other records required by environmental statutes and/or regulations.

I'm a journalist with the nonprofit newsroom Sentient. Any responsive documents will be used in reporting relating to public health, safety, and environmental concerns in the public interest.

In the case that the response to this is voluminous, I would appreciate being provided records in installments to expedite the process.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Grey Moran

communications.subject: Receipt of EPA National FOIA Office FOIA Request
communications.communication:
Dear Grey  Moran, 

Thank you for submitting your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request. 
Case Number 2025-EPA-08943 has been assigned to this request. In all future correspondence regarding this request, please reference your case number. 
If you selected EPA National FOIA Office (for all non-OIG requests), your request was transmitted directly to the EPA’s National FOIA Office (NFO). NFO will confirm receipt and assignment of your request by sending you an Acknowledgement email within five business days. 
If you have any questions, please contact the FOIA Requester Service Center at hq.foia@epa.gov (mailto:hq.foia@epa.gov) or at 202-566-1667.
Regards, 
EPA National FOIA Office

communications.subject: Need More Information - EPA FOIA Request Number 2025-EPA-08943
communications.communication:
September 16, 2025 

Grey  Moran 

MuckRock News DEPT MR 192998, 263 Huntington Ave 
Boston, MA  02115 
Emailed to: 192998-61310190@requests.muckrock.com 
RE: Need More Information - EPA FOIA Request Number 2025-EPA-08943 

Hello Grey  Moran: 

This email concerns the above-referenced Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request, submitted to the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), National FOIA Office (NFO) on September 15, 2025. You are seeking the following: "I hereby request the following records:-A log of complaints, filed to the Environmental Protection Agency between January 1st, 2025 and the date of response to this request, concerning the suspected tampering, misrepresentation, or otherwise altering of documents, permits, certifications, or any other records required by environmental statutes and/or regulations." 

After review of your FOIA request, we determined your request does not reasonably describe the records you are seeking in a way that will permit EPA employees to identify and locate them. As submitted, you are asking for a whole of agency review of millions of documents submitted to EPA. This request is overly broad and does not provide us with any specific Office or program for which to begin a reasonable search for records. Additionally, the contact phone number you provided was invalid and we will need to discuss this request at length by phone. The indicated "log of complaints" is not a document. 

EPA’s FOIA regulations state: “Whenever possible, your request should include specific information about each record sought, such as the date, title or name, author, recipient, and subject matter. If known, the requester should include any file designations or descriptions for the records that the requester wants. The more specific the requester is about the records or type of records that the requester wants, the more likely EPA will be able to identify and locate records responsive to the request.” 40 C.F.R 2.102 (c) The FOIA does not require an agency to create new records, answer questions posed by requesters, or attempt to interpret a request that does not identify reasonably described records. 

We would like to provide you with the opportunity to clarify the records that you are seeking so that EPA can process your request.  As indicated in 40 C.F.R. § 2.102(c), please include any specific information about the records, including time period, authors, or a more detailed description of the records' subject matter.  Please contact Larry Johnson at johnson.larry-c@epa.gov with your clarification or let us know if youwould like to schedule a time to discuss your request over the phone. 

Please reply within 20 calendar days from the date of this email by October 10, 2025 .  If we do not hear back from you, EPA may issue a final determination to close out your request. Please note that you are not prohibited from submitting additional Freedom of Information Act requests in the future. 
For more information, please visit our website at: https://www.epa.gov/foia. If you need any further assistance or would like to discuss any aspect of your request, you may seek assistance from EPA’s FOIA Public Liaison at hq.foia@epa.gov or call (202) 566-1667. You may also seek assistance from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS). You may contact OGIS in any of the following ways: by mail, Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road, College Park, MD 20740-6001; email: ogis@nara.gov; telephone: (202) 741-5770 or (877) 684-6448; or fax: (202) 741-5769.  We look forward to your prompt reply. 
Sincerely, 
National FOIA Office 
U.S. Environmental Protection Agency

communications.subject: FOIA Request 2025-EPA-08943
communications.communication:
Hello Grey - The above request has been assigned to EPA's Office of Civil Enforcement for a response. However, depending on your specific interests, other offices may be better suited to responding to your request. I'm writing to gather additional information that may inform decisions with respect to which of our offices may be the recipients of the tips and complaints in which you're interested. My understanding is that during interactions with my Office of General Counsel colleague, Larry Johnson, you indicated you're specifically interested in those received through the Agency's Report a Violation or RAV web platform. I would greatly appreciate receiving confirmation if this is the case. If your interests are broader, please call me at the number below or reply to this message with a phone number and a few windows of availability and I'll reach out to you.

I'm looking forward to your email reply and/or call.

Regards,

Royan

communications.subject: FOIA 2025-EPA-08943 (Moran)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr./Ms. Moran

By this letter, EPA hereby requests an extension to April 15, 2026, to respond to your above-referenced Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request, due to unusual circumstances.

The unusual circumstances are as follows:


  1.  EPA has encountered difficulties exporting the reports from the Report a Violation database.  The database is quite old and does not possess the functionality that modern databases possess.  For that reason, EPA is in the process of modernizing the Report a Violation database to address the current database's shortcomings.


  1.  Exporting the documents is necessary for document reviewers to see the documents, determine which documents are responsive, and determine whether any FOIA exemptions apply that would justify withholding any information.


  1.  EPA has identified that there are over 1,700 potentially responsive documents.  After the documents are exported, EPA anticipates that reviewing this large number of documents will take 90 days.


  1.  Determining which documents are responsive, determining whether any FOIA exemptions apply that would justify withholding any information, and removing information that should be withheld will take 90 days.

EPA will take taking steps to provide you with responsive documents as expeditiously as possible and doing so may involve providing you with what are called interim releases, which are releases of some but not all responsive documents while document review continues.

Please respond to this email within two (2) business days to confirm your receipt of this email and to confirm your acceptance of EPA's requested extension.

Please feel free to contact me if you have any question.

Thank you.


Claude Earl Walker
Deputy Director, Legal Counsel Division
U.S. Environmental Protection Agency
Office of Criminal Enforcement, Forensics & Training
1200 Pennsylvania Ave., N.W.
WJC South Room 1202C (Mail Code 2232A)
Washington, D.C. 20004
(202) 207-8603 (phone)

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2025-EPA-08943
communications.communication:
Dear Claude,

Thank you for your attention to this. and providing this update. Yes, I agree to the extension of April 15, 2026. I would also  appreciate if you could provide me with interim releases as batches of documents are ready. 

Best,
Grey Moran
(917-405-8270)

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Crawford County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Crawford County)
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com
