title: Sheriff Detention (Oswego County Sheriffs Office)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff Detention (Oswego County Sheriffs Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

- The most recent contract, including any amendments and modifications, between your office and U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and/ or the U.S. Marshals for the purpose of allowing ICE to utilize bed space in your jail.
- Records relating annual inspection of the facility conducted in 2023, 2024, and 2025 by the U.S. Department Homeland Security. These records should include the full annual inspection report and any documents or communications regarding the inspection.
- Records relating any other local, state, or federal inspections of the facility conducted in 2023, 2024, and 2025.
- Any ICE Enforcement and Removal Operations Facility Significant Incident Summary (SIS) produced between January 1, 2023, and the date this request is processed.

Since January 1, 2023:
- Communications, including electronic communications and attached documents, between your department and ICE and/or the U.S. Marshals related to the contract. This should include any communications and documents relating to requests, approvals, or denials of waivers of contract provisions.
- Billings, invoices, and records of payment made pursuant to the contract.
- Monthly records on the average daily population and average length of detention of people detained in ICE custody at the jail.
- Complaints or grievances filed by people detained in ICE custody at the jail.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Julia Rock

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: UC Berkeley Public Records Log
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the 2025 log of requests made to UC Berkeley.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Mitch Poran

title: Chauntae Davies investigatory records

communications.subject: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: Chauntae Davies investigatory records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Chauntae Davies, an alleged victim of Jeffrey Epstein, has stated that she cooperated with the New Mexico Attorney General's Office starting in 2019. Please provide any records related to Chauntae Davies' engagement with the Office beginning January 1, 2019 to January 1, 2022. Please include any records related to Chauntae Davies and Jeffrey Epstein, Ghislaine Maxwell, and the Zorro Ranch, located near Stanley, New Mexico.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Tracey

communications.subject: Your New Mexico Department of Justice public records request #25-498 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
New Mexico Department of Justice ************************************************************************ Your record request #25-498 has been submitted successfully. This is an automated message. Thank you for submitting your request to the New Mexico Department of Justice. We will review your request and begin processing it. Typically, you will receive two communications from us, one within three business days and the second within 15 days. If we need additional time to process your request, we will let you know in our 15 day communication. If you have any documents relevant to your records request, please upload them or provide copies to us to assist with our searches. Please also let us know if you are looking for an exact document within our records so that we may provide it to you more quickly. We strive to complete requests as timely and completely as we can and your assistance will help us in this process. If you need to contact us, you may reply to this message. ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact New Mexico Department of Justice.

Change your email settings | Visit our help center

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Whatcom County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Whatcom County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Sheriff Records Request :: S000120-012926
communications.communication:
Dear Samuel Sinyangwe: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Whatcom County Sheriff's Office. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 1/29/2026 and given the reference number S000120-012926 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2202249/ 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Whatcom County Sheriff's Office

communications.subject: WCSO PDR S000120-012926
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon,

Please find the records you requested from our agency attached (PDR Reference S000120-012926). I am emailing them to you as they did not format correctly when uploaded into the GovQA portal. All responsive records have been provided and this request is now closed.

Please note: The PRA's one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run and we do not intend to further address this request. If you have questions or need further assistance, please contact our records unit.

Regards,

[cid:image003.png@01DCCE7F.3C2A8420]
Hannah Zabel, CPES

title: Police disciplinary files (Walla Walla County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Police disciplinary files (Walla Walla County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Any and all lists, spreadsheets or other organized datasets that detail potential or substantiated allegations of misconduct and/or impeached or impeachable testimony, created under Brady v. Maryland and/or Giglio v. United States.
- Any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Moe Clark

This letter serves as Walla Walla County’s initial five-day letter, pursuant to RCW 42.56.520, to your public records request. After your request is reviewed for content and we determine the department or office in which your request refers to, the County will provide an estimate to provide an initial installment of responsive records. After locating responsive records, the County will determine whether any of the responsive documents are subject to applicable exemptions under the Public Records Act, and to notify any third parties affected by the request. If third party notification is required, twenty (20) business days will be allowed for a response by the third party to those records which specifically pertain to them. title: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request – Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243

Dear Open Records Officer,

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., I respectfully request access to the following public administrative and clerical records maintained by the Office of the Clerk of State Court concerning Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243 in DeKalb County State Court and/or Magistrate Court, as applicable:
1. Deputy Clerk Identities
Names and titles of all deputy clerks or Clerk’s Office personnel who processed, reviewed, docketed, rejected, closed, dismissed, or otherwise handled filings or records in these cases.

2. Digital and Administrative Records
For each filing in the above cases, I request:
Any internal audit logs, access logs, activity histories, or other system-generated records maintained by the Clerk’s Office within any electronic case management, e-filing, or document management system used to receive, review, process, docket, store, or manage filings, identifying all users who accessed or took administrative action on those filings.

Metadata associated with filings and docket entries, including timestamps, user actions, status changes, and internal notes.

Internal clerk review annotations, comments, or administrative correspondence associated with each filing.

Any administrative records generated in the course of processing, docketing, closing, dismissing, or maintaining these case records.

Copies of all filings, docket entries, and case status records sufficient to ensure a complete and accurate administrative record, including any updates or amendments.

3.  Administrative Policies and Training Materials
Written policies, procedures, or training materials governing the processing, docketing, dismissal, closure, and record maintenance of State Court and Magistrate Court matters, including procedures applicable when a defendant does not appear.

4.  Scope and Limitations
This request is limited to administrative and ministerial records and public documentation. It does not seek confidential judicial deliberations, legal analysis, or privileged attorney communications.

If any portion of a responsive record is claimed to be exempt from disclosure, please produce all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions with redactions as permitted by law and identify the specific statutory basis for each redaction or withholding, pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(d).

5. Purpose and System Applicability 
The purpose of this request is to identify the personnel responsible for processing these filings and to obtain a complete and accurate administrative and digital record of how these matters were handled and maintained by the Clerk’s Office.

This request applies to any electronic system or database used by the Clerk’s Office to receive, process, review, manage, or store filings for these cases, regardless of vendor or system name.

6. Format and Fees
If any fees are associated with producing these records, please notify me prior to processing. I request that the records be provided in electronic format, if available.

Thank you for your attention to this request. 

Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

Subject: Second Request and Notice of Noncompliance – Georgia Open Records Act

Dear Open Records Officer,

This message follows my Georgia Open Records Act request submitted on 12/27/2025 via the MuckRock Foundation concerning administrative records related to Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243.

As of today, more than three business days have elapsed since the request was received. No response, acknowledgment, production, denial, or notice of extension has been provided.

Under the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1)(A), an agency is required within three business days to produce the requested records, provide a timetable for production, or issue a written denial citing the specific legal exemptions relied upon. A failure to respond constitutes noncompliance with the Act.

Accordingly, this email serves as a restatement of the original request and as notice that the Office is currently out of compliance with GORA. 

Please provide:
	1	The requested records; or
	2	A written response identifying when responsive records will be produced; or
	3	A written denial identifying the specific statutory exemptions relied upon for any records withheld.

If the Office contends that no responsive records exist, please confirm that determination in writing.

I respectfully request that the Office respond within five (5) business days to cure this noncompliance. If the matter is not resolved, I will consider pursuing the remedies available under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-73, including referral to the Georgia Attorney General’s Office or judicial enforcement.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

Provide a timetable for production, or

Issue a written denial citing specific statutory exemptions for any records withheld.

The previous forwarding to another department does not satisfy this statutory obligation. I respectfully request that the Clerk’s Office respond directly with the requested records, a production timeline, or a written denial citing exemptions.

If the office does not respond substantively within this timeframe, I may pursue the remedies available under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-73, including referral to the Georgia Attorney General or judicial enforcement.

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr. , pro se

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Rodriguez,

I am writing to follow up on my Georgia Open Records Act request, originally submitted on December 27, 2025, regarding Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243, and subsequent correspondence, including my March 17, 2026 follow-up requesting a more definite production timeline and a general description of responsive record categories.

As of today, I have not received a substantive update regarding the status of this request or any information regarding the search, identification, or anticipated production of records. Given the upcoming trial in this matter, scheduled for April 6–8, 2026, it is important to have a clear understanding of the status and availability of administrative and clerical records maintained by the Clerk’s Office.

To ensure compliance with O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq. and to facilitate proper documentation, I respectfully request the following information at your earliest convenience:
	
1	Categories of Responsive Records Identified – A general description of the types of administrative and clerical records your office has identified as responsive to this request.
	
2	Search Scope and Methodology – A description of which offices, departments, electronic systems, and personnel are reviewing records for this request, including the procedures, criteria, or keywords applied in identifying responsive records.
	
3	Records Determination – Clarification regarding any records that may be withheld, partially withheld, or redacted, along with the statutory basis for any such exemptions.
	
4	Updated Production Timeline – A specific estimated timeframe for the production of records identified as responsive, considering the current stage of review.

Providing this information will help ensure the request is processed thoroughly and will allow for alignment with ongoing trial preparation. Documentation of these details is consistent with the transparency objectives of the Georgia Open Records Act and will assist in maintaining a clear record of the Clerk’s Office’s processing of this request.

I respectfully request a response no later than March 31, 2026, so that any available information can be considered in advance of the April 6 trial.

Thank you for your attention to this matter and your continued cooperation.

Sincerely,
/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243
communications.communication:
Final Demand and Notice of Noncompliance – Georgia Open Records Act Request (Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243) 

Dear Ms. Rodriguez,

This correspondence serves as a final demand regarding my Georgia Open Records Act request originally submitted on December 27, 2025, concerning administrative and clerical records for Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243.

This request remains directed to the DeKalb County State Court Clerk’s Office as the legal custodian of the requested records.

As you are aware, the Georgia Open Records Act requires that an agency, within three (3) business days of receipt, either produce responsive records, provide a specific timetable for production, or issue a written denial identifying the legal basis for withholding records. See O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1)(A).

To date, more than three months have elapsed. No records have been produced, no legally sufficient denial has been issued, and no definite production schedule has been provided. Repeated statements indicating that “10–14 business days” are needed, without actual production or firm dates, do not satisfy the requirements of the Act.

Additionally, this request concerns administrative and clerical records maintained by the Clerk’s Office and is not limited to, nor properly routed through, the Dispossessory Department. Continued routing of this request to an unrelated division has contributed to delay and noncompliance.

Please also be advised that these records are directly relevant to ongoing litigation involving the handling, processing, and administrative management of the referenced cases. Accordingly, you are under a duty to preserve all responsive records, including but not limited to electronic records, audit logs, metadata, internal communications, and any system-generated data reflecting access or activity.

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby demand that within three (3) business days you provide:
Immediate production of all responsive records currently available;
A definite, good-faith production schedule with specific calendar dates for any remaining records; or
A written denial identifying each withheld record or category and the specific statutory exemption relied upon.

Failure to comply will result in pursuit of all available remedies under the Georgia Open Records Act, including enforcement proceedings, as well as appropriate use of this record of noncompliance in related litigation.

I remain willing to work cooperatively to facilitate prompt resolution of this matter.

Sincerely, 

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Concord School District/SAU8)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: Open Records Request – Council Member Communications with Flock Safety

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request – Council Member Communications with Flock Safety
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All emails, text messages, direct messages, or written communications of any kind between John Heneghan of the Dunwoody City Council and any employee, representative, agent, or contractor of Flock Safety, from December 1, 2025 through the date this request is received.

Please provide all responsive records in their original electronic, machine-readable format, including full metadata, headers, and any attachments.

Please do not redirect me to a transparency portal or dashboard in lieu of producing the underlying records. This request is made for non-commercial purposes, and the responsive records will be made available to the general public. If any portion of the requested records is withheld or redacted, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon. If there are any anticipated costs, please notify me in advance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: Flock Safety / ALPR Surveillance Records – Town of East Fishkill

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: FOIL Request – Flock Safety / ALPR Surveillance Records – Town of East Fishkill
communications.communication:
Dear Records Custodian,

Under the provisions of the New York Freedom of Information Law (FOIL), N.Y. Public Officers Law §§ 84–90, I hereby request access to and copies of records held by the Town of East Fishkill, Dutchess County, relating to the planning, proposal, procurement, authorization, or attempted deployment of Flock Safety surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems.

This request is made in the public interest to better understand the use, consideration, or implementation of surveillance technologies by local government entities, including decision-making processes, vendor relationships, and potential impacts on public privacy.

The purpose of this request is to determine the origin, authorization process, procurement activity, proposed locations, and communications surrounding any surveillance camera system, including identifying which individual, agency, or vendor first initiated discussions or planning for such a program within the Town of East Fishkill.

Timeframe: January 1, 2023 – Present, including any earlier records referencing or relating to Flock Safety, automated license plate readers, or surveillance cameras within the Town of East Fishkill.

Records Requested

For the purpose of this request, the records I seek include, but are not limited to the following:

1. Contracts and Agreements

All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, purchase orders, vendor agreements, procurement documents, or proposals involving the Town of East Fishkill and Flock Safety or any related entities concerning surveillance cameras, automated license plate readers, or related monitoring technology.

This includes draft contracts, executed agreements, amendments, pilot program agreements, trial program documentation, cancellation notices, procurement justifications, and contract termination records.

Legal Basis: Contracts involving public funds or public infrastructure are public records subject to disclosure under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87.

2. Records Identifying Project Initiation

All records reflecting the initial introduction, proposal, solicitation, or outreach regarding Flock Safety cameras or ALPR systems, including records identifying:

• the individual or office that first proposed or recommended the system  
• the first communications between the Town and Flock Safety  
• vendor outreach to town officials  
• internal discussions about introducing the technology  
• marketing or sales outreach from vendors  

3. Internal Communications

All internal communications including emails, memoranda, text messages, chat messages, letters, meeting notes, or other correspondence among:

• the Town Supervisor  
• Town Board members  
• Town of East Fishkill Police Department personnel  
• Town administrative staff  
• Town legal counsel  
• Highway or Building Department personnel  
• other municipal offices  

that discuss:

• Flock Safety  
• automated license plate readers  
• surveillance cameras  
• camera deployments  
• vendor procurement or negotiations  
• surveillance technology adoption  

4. Communications With External Entities

All communications between the Town of East Fishkill and:

• representatives of Flock Safety  
• technology vendors or consultants  
• Dutchess County officials  
• local, state, or federal law enforcement agencies  
• regional intelligence or real-time crime centers  

concerning surveillance cameras, ALPR systems, or the Flock Safety program.

5. Vendor Demonstrations and Sales Outreach

All records relating to vendor presentations, product demonstrations, marketing materials, or sales outreach regarding Flock Safety or related surveillance technology, including:

• presentation decks  
• vendor proposals  
• demonstration scheduling emails  
• meeting invitations or call summaries  
• webinar invitations  
• follow-up communications  

6. Proposed Camera Locations and Site Selection

All records identifying, evaluating, or discussing proposed or potential locations for surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader systems within the Town of East Fishkill.

This includes but is not limited to:

• maps showing proposed camera placements  
• GIS maps or infrastructure maps  
• deployment plans  
• proposed installation locations or intersections  
• roadway monitoring points  
• entry or exit routes to the town  
• communications discussing site selection  
• vendor recommendations for placement  
• field surveys or site inspections  
• infrastructure or engineering assessments related to camera placement  

7. Deployment and Planning Documents

All planning or operational records relating to the surveillance system, including:

• deployment plans  
• camera location maps  
• infrastructure assessments  
• equipment specifications  
• installation schedules  
• operational capability descriptions  
• technology architecture documents  

8. Financial and Procurement Records

All records relating to funding, budgeting, or procurement of the surveillance system, including:

• invoices  
• payment records  
• purchase authorizations  
• budget allocations  
• grant applications  
• cost analyses  
• procurement justifications  
• financial approvals  

9. Meeting Records

All agendas, minutes, recordings, transcripts, or notes from Town Board meetings, committee meetings, departmental meetings, or internal meetings where surveillance camera programs or Flock Safety systems were discussed, proposed, approved, or canceled.

10. Oversight, Audit, and Compliance Records

Any audit reports, legal opinions, compliance reviews, investigations, or internal analyses relating to surveillance camera programs or procurement processes.

11. Data Access, Sharing, and Integration

All records relating to data access or sharing involving the surveillance system, including:

• agreements allowing other agencies to access camera data  
• policies governing license plate reader searches  
• records concerning integration with regional surveillance networks  
• data retention policies  
• privacy or civil liberties assessments  

Electronic Records and Metadata

For all responsive electronic records, including emails and digital documents, I request that records be produced in their native electronic format where possible, including all available metadata and email header information.

This includes, but is not limited to:

• sender and recipient information  
• CC and BCC fields  
• full email header routing data  
• timestamps for sending and receipt  
• message ID fields  
• attachment metadata  
• document creation dates  
• last modified dates  
• author fields  
• file path information where available  

Providing metadata is necessary to determine the origin, timing, and authorship of communications relating to the surveillance camera proposal.

Request for Segregable Non-Exempt Portions

Should any portion of the requested records be deemed exempt from disclosure, I request the release of any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions in accordance with N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87(2).

Preservation of Records

Please note your obligation not to destroy or alter any records potentially responsive to this request. Once a FOIL request has been received, agencies are required to preserve all responsive records during processing.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a fee waiver for the processing of this request as the information sought is in the public interest and its disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of government surveillance programs and decision-making processes.

If potential fees exceed $200, please inform me before proceeding.

Public Availability of Communications

Please be advised that communications relating to this request, including incoming and outgoing correspondence, may be publicly published on transparency platforms such as MuckRock.com.

Statutory Response Time

As required under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 89(3), I look forward to acknowledgment of this request within five business days.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter  
Independent Investigative Journalist

files.source: Jordan Lassiter

• the individual or office that first proposed or recommended the system
• the first communications between the Town and Flock Safety
• vendor outreach to town officials
• internal discussions about introducing the technology
• marketing or sales outreach from vendors

3. Internal Communications

All internal communications including emails, memoranda, text messages, chat messages, letters, meeting notes, or other correspondence among:

• the Town Supervisor
• Town Board members
• Town of East Fishkill Police Department personnel
• Town administrative staff
• Town legal counsel
• Highway or Building Department personnel
• other municipal offices

that discuss:

• Flock Safety
• automated license plate readers
• surveillance cameras
• camera deployments
• vendor procurement or negotiations
• surveillance technology adoption

4. Communications With External Entities

All communications between the Town of East Fishkill and:

• representatives of Flock Safety
• technology vendors or consultants
• Dutchess County officials
• local, state, or federal law enforcement agencies
• regional intelligence or real-time crime centers

concerning surveillance cameras, ALPR systems, or the Flock Safety program.

5. Vendor Demonstrations and Sales Outreach

All records relating to vendor presentations, product demonstrations, marketing materials, or sales outreach regarding Flock Safety or related surveillance technology, including:

• presentation decks
• vendor proposals
• demonstration scheduling emails
• meeting invitations or call summaries
• webinar invitations
• follow-up communications

6. Proposed Camera Locations and Site Selection

All records identifying, evaluating, or discussing proposed or potential locations for surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader systems within the Town of East Fishkill.

This includes but is not limited to:

• maps showing proposed camera placements
• GIS maps or infrastructure maps
• deployment plans
• proposed installation locations or intersections
• roadway monitoring points
• entry or exit routes to the town
• communications discussing site selection
• vendor recommendations for placement
• field surveys or site inspections
• infrastructure or engineering assessments related to camera placement

7. Deployment and Planning Documents

All planning or operational records relating to the surveillance system, including:

• deployment plans
• camera location maps
• infrastructure assessments
• equipment specifications
• installation schedules
• operational capability descriptions
• technology architecture documents

8. Financial and Procurement Records

All records relating to funding, budgeting, or procurement of the surveillance system, including:

• invoices
• payment records
• purchase authorizations
• budget allocations
• grant applications
• cost analyses
• procurement justifications
• financial approvals

9. Meeting Records

All agendas, minutes, recordings, transcripts, or notes from Town Board meetings, committee meetings, departmental meetings, or internal meetings where surveillance camera programs or Flock Safety systems were discussed, proposed, approved, or canceled.

10. Oversight, Audit, and Compliance Records

Any audit reports, legal opinions, compliance reviews, investigations, or internal analyses relating to surveillance camera programs or procurement processes.

11. Data Access, Sharing, and Integration

All records relating to data access or sharing involving the surveillance system, including:

• agreements allowing other agencies to access camera data
• policies governing license plate reader searches
• records concerning integration with regional surveillance networks
• data retention policies
• privacy or civil liberties assessments

Electronic Records and Metadata

For all responsive electronic records, including emails and digital documents, I request that records be produced in their native electronic format where possible, including all available metadata and email header information.

This includes, but is not limited to:

• sender and recipient information
• CC and BCC fields
• full email header routing data
• timestamps for sending and receipt
• message ID fields
• attachment metadata
• document creation dates
• last modified dates
• author fields
• file path information where available

Providing metadata is necessary to determine the origin, timing, and authorship of communications relating to the surveillance camera proposal.

Request for Segregable Non-Exempt Portions

Should any portion of the requested records be deemed exempt from disclosure, I request the release of any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions in accordance with N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87(2).

Preservation of Records

Please note your obligation not to destroy or alter any records potentially responsive to this request. Once a FOIL request has been received, agencies are required to preserve all responsive records during processing.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a fee waiver for the processing of this request as the information sought is in the public interest and its disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of government surveillance programs and decision-making processes.

If potential fees exceed $200, please inform me before proceeding.

Public Availability of Communications

Please be advised that communications relating to this request, including incoming and outgoing correspondence, may be publicly published on transparency platforms such as MuckRock.com.

Statutory Response Time

As required under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 89(3), I look forward to acknowledgment of this request within five business days.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter
Independent Investigative Journalist

title: Request to Navy for 2018 budget report of Public Affairs Office

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Navy for 2019 budget report of Public Affairs Office
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The annual budget report for the Navy's Public Affairs Office for the year 2018.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

Respectfully,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Your FOIA request 2026-USMCFOIA-001847
communications.communication:
Good morning Mr. Carollo, 

This is to acknowledge the receipt of your request and to let you know that I am the analyst assigned for processing.  If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to me via email. 

Please be aware that, whenever a FOIA requester has not received a determination within 20 working days from the date a request is perfected, they are entitled to challenge the delay.  To do this, you may submit an appeal within the SecureRelease online portal (www.securerelease.us) or by writing to the Department of the Navy, Office of the Judge Advocate General (Code 14), 1322 Patterson Ave SE, Suite 3000, Washington Navy Yard, DC 20374-5066.  If you prefer, you can also submit an appeal via email to Navy-OJAG-C14-FOIA-PA-ADMINAPPEALS@us.navy.mil.  Your appeal, if any, should include a statement indicating why you believe it should be granted.  It must be postmarked or timestamped within 90 calendar days from the date of this letter. 

Requesters also have the right to seek assistance and/or dispute resolution services from the Department of the Navy FOIA Public Liaison, Mr. Christopher Julka, at Christopher.a.julka.civ@us.navy.mil or (703) 697-0031, and may also contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) for assistance and/or dispute resolution at ogis@nara.gov or (877) 684-6448. 

Thank you for your patience as we work to complete the processing of your request.  Hope you have a great day. 

Sincerely, 
Tara M. Walters 

Commandant of the Marine Corps (ARSF) 
Headquarters US Marine Corps 
3000 Marine Corps Pentagon Rm 2B289 
Washington DC 20350-3000 

FOIA/PA Phone:  (703) 614-4008  DSN:  224 
Fax:  (703) 614-6287 
FOIA Email:  hqmcfoia@usmc.mil
